name: General Practice (GP) Services
description: Routine healthcare, scripts, vaccinations, preventive care and specialist referrals.
source_summary: GP care is a core entry point for Victorian healthcare services.

name: After-hours medical services
description: Out-of-hours medical advice and non-emergency clinic support when your regular GP is unavailable.
source_summary: Victoria outlines after-hours options for urgent but non-emergency care.

name: Victorian Urgent Care Clinic
description: Urgent but non-life-threatening illness or injury with no appointment or referral needed.
source_summary: Urgent care clinics are for urgent, non-life-threatening conditions.

name: Chemist Care Now
description: Participating pharmacies provide treatment for selected conditions without needing a GP appointment.
source_summary: Chemist Care Now offers fast care pathways for selected lower-acuity conditions.

name: Community Health Centre services
description: Local multidisciplinary services including nursing, allied health, counselling and chronic condition support.
source_summary: Community health centres deliver place-based multidisciplinary care in Victoria.

name: Physiotherapy services
description: Rehabilitation and movement support for injury, pain and functional recovery.
source_summary: Physiotherapy is a common allied health pathway in community and private settings.

name: Occupational therapy services
description: Support for independence in daily activities, home setup and functional participation.
source_summary: OT services support people with functional limitations and everyday participation goals.

name: Speech pathology services
description: Assessment and therapy for speech, language, communication and swallowing concerns.
source_summary: Speech pathology is part of allied health care across age groups.

name: Dietitian services
description: Nutrition assessment and diet planning for chronic conditions and preventive health.
source_summary: Dietitian support is available through allied health and primary care pathways.

name: Podiatry services
description: Foot and lower-limb care including pain, mobility and diabetes-related foot health.
source_summary: Podiatry is a frequent allied health pathway for mobility and chronic foot issues.

name: NDIS access and eligibility pathway
description: Pathway for people seeking NDIS access based on age, residency and disability-related eligibility criteria.
source_summary: NDIS supports must relate to disability and meet access and support criteria under NDIS rules.

name: NDIS therapy supports
description: Evidence-based therapy supports delivered by qualified allied health professionals to build independence and participation.
source_summary: Therapy supports are funded when they are disability-related and meet NDIS support requirements.

name: NDIS disability-related health and daily supports
description: Disability-related health supports (for example continence, wound care or dysphagia), assistive technology and daily living supports.
source_summary: Disability-related health supports can be funded when they relate to functional impact and disability support goals.

name: NDIS behaviour support
description: Positive behaviour support pathways for participants with behaviours of concern, including specialist assessment and planning.
source_summary: Behaviour support is a recognised NDIS support type under official guidance.

name: NDIS social and recreation supports
description: Supports that help participants build social connection, community participation and recreational engagement.
source_summary: Social and recreation supports are included in NDIS guideline materials.

name: NDIS work and employment supports
description: Support to help participants pursue work goals, including job preparation and workplace support pathways.
source_summary: Supports in employment are covered in official NDIS supports guidance.

name: NDIS supported independent living pathways
description: Housing and daily living support pathways including supported independent living and related in-home assistance.
source_summary: Supported independent living and home supports are part of NDIS support pathways.

name: My Aged Care assessment
description: Entry assessment for government-subsidised aged care services.
source_summary: Assessment-first process for aged care eligibility and support planning.

name: Home care package providers
description: In-home aged care services such as personal care, domestic help and nursing support.
source_summary: Home care packages support older people to remain safely at home.

name: Residential aged care homes
description: 24/7 residential support and nursing for older people with higher care needs.
source_summary: Residential care pathway for people who can no longer live safely at home.

name: Respite aged care
description: Short-term care options to support carers and provide temporary cover.
source_summary: Respite pathways provide short-term care when regular carers need a break.

name: headspace youth mental health support
description: Mental health support for young people through centres and online services.
source_summary: headspace targets young people aged 12 to 25 for mental health support.

name: Mental Health and Wellbeing Locals (26+)
description: Community mental health and wellbeing services for adults aged 26 years and over.
source_summary: Free Victorian community mental health and wellbeing support for adults 26+.

name: Public mental health and counselling
description: GP and community pathways for people needing counselling, assessment and treatment support.
source_summary: Mental health treatment plan pathway is linked to diagnosis, GP assessment and referral options.

name: Crisis Assessment and Treatment (CAT)
description: 24/7 crisis response for acute mental health emergencies and safety concerns.
source_summary: Victoria includes crisis services for urgent mental health episodes.

name: Alcohol and other drug treatment
description: Treatment programs for alcohol or drug dependence, including counselling and rehab pathways.
source_summary: Victoria provides alcohol and other drug support pathways through treatment and counselling services.

name: DirectLine alcohol and drug counselling
description: 24/7 confidential phone counselling for alcohol and other drug issues.
source_summary: DirectLine is part of Victoria's alcohol and drug support system.

name: Mobile women's health clinic
description: Free women’s health outreach for women, girls and gender-diverse people in regional and rural Victoria.
source_summary: Mobile clinic model increases access in regional and rural Victoria.

name: Cervical screening and preventive women’s health
description: Cervical screening, preventive checks and women-focused primary care support.
source_summary: Cervical screening and preventive women’s care are core women’s health pathways in Victoria.

name: Contraception and reproductive planning
description: Contraception advice, reproductive planning and related women’s health consultations.
source_summary: Contraception and reproductive planning are common women’s health service pathways.

name: Family planning and sexual health clinic
description: Contraception, sexual health checks, STI screening and reproductive counselling.
source_summary: Sexual and reproductive health pathways include family planning and STI services.

name: HIV prevention and sexual health advice
description: Prevention-focused sexual health care including PrEP/PEP advice and risk-reduction support.
source_summary: HIV prevention and sexual health advice are standard components of sexual health service delivery.

name: Men's health clinic
description: Targeted men's health support including screening, sexual health and preventive checks.
source_summary: Healthdirect outlines men's health topics and support pathways.

name: Men’s sexual and reproductive health
description: Male-focused sexual and reproductive health support including screening and preventive care.
source_summary: Men’s sexual and reproductive health is an established men’s health care pathway.

name: Aboriginal health service
description: Culturally safe, community-controlled holistic care for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.
source_summary: Aboriginal health services provide culturally safe community-led care.

name: Aboriginal women's health clinic
description: Specialised culturally safe women’s health support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander women.
source_summary: Victoria lists dedicated Aboriginal women's health pathways.
